title: Sustaining success across generations
desc: Family-owned businesses have long been the backbone of Thailand's economy, quietly powering growth, creating jobs and building wealth across generations. Yet beneath this strength lies a fragile reality, as most family enterprises struggle to survive beyond their founders.

title: BBL recommends Thai businesses mull expansion to India
desc: Bangkok Bank (BBL) is encouraging Thai businesses to expand into India to reduce their reliance on the US market amid rising geopolitical risk and escalating global trade tensions.

title: Pet products remain on clear growth trajectory
desc: The local pet industry is expected to remain resilient over the long term, as many owners treat their pets as family members. Over the past decade, the sector has recorded strong growth.

title: Dining sector poised for downturn
desc: Restaurants are bracing for less vibrant sales during the Songkran holiday this year, which runs from April 11-15, according to the Thai Restaurant Association.

title: Toyota to chat with authorities on oil, power
desc: Toyota Motor Thailand is preparing to hold talks with the new government on oil and electricity policies, as surging energy prices weigh heavily on consumers considering car purchases.

title: 36 migrants found in hiding
desc: KANCHANABURI: Police arrested 36 individuals, including a local man, for harbouring undocumented foreign workers.

title: Russian tour guide arrested
desc: PHUKET: Tourist police arrested a Russian national for illegally offering tour services on a Destination Thailand Visa (DTV).

title: Tourists 'must be insured' before entering
desc: Thailand is pushing for regulations that would require international tourists to obtain accident insurance before entering the country, as rising unpaid medical bills and accident risks place a mounting strain on public healthcare.

title: Rajavithi completes first robotic heart operation
desc: Rajavithi Hospital has completed the country's first robotic heart surgery, marking a major advance in minimally invasive cardiac care.

title: Contested Clean Air Bill 'still worth a go'
desc: Severe haze in northern Thailand has intensified pressure on the government to adopt stronger legal tools, including advancing the contested Clean Air Bill.

title: Oman envoy talks Mideast  woes with tourist police
desc: Tourist Police and the Omani Embassy have held talks aimed at strengthening tourist safety, deepening bilateral cooperation and addressing the impact of Middle East tensions on travel confidence and regional transport.

title: Hoteliers call for more direct flights
desc: The Thai Hotels Association's Southern Chapter has urged the authorities to increase direct flights from Europe to Phuket to stabilise tourism amid travel disruptions caused by the Middle East war.

title: Anutin plans to present new measures to boost economy
desc: The government is preparing urgent economic and policy measures ahead of its parliamentary statement, aiming to boost growth, ease living costs and steer Thailand out of the middle income trap.

title: Extreme heat to scorch Northern, Central regions
desc: Temperatures could hit 42C this week as the Meteorological Department warns of scattered thunderstorms and gusty winds.

title: Oil depot reported for hoarding
desc: SURAT THANI: The provincial commerce office has filed a police complaint against an oil depot operator for allegedly hoarding controlled fuel, as authorities ramp up the crackdown on fuel stockpiling and smuggling in the South and along border areas.

title: No Brand brings standalone  K-grocery to Central Bangna
desc: No Brand, the renowned South Korean grocery store, recently launched its first standalone store in Thailand at Central Bangna, bringing thousands of products and introducing K-value retail experience for smart shoppers.

title: Asia Pillars offers 'Open Doors' C-suite networking at Park Hyatt Bangkok
desc: Asia Pillars is holding "Open Doors", an exclusive business networking event for non-members to connect and engage with influential business leaders, at Penthouse Bar+Grill of Park Hyatt Bangkok, Witthayu Road, on April 21 at 6pm.

title: Diesel surges past B50/litre
desc: Diesel prices have surged past 50 baht per litre, pushing up costs across the transport, tourism and agriculture sectors and prompting many Thais to either cancel or scale back their Songkran travel plans.

title: Navy disciplines officer for assaulting conscript
desc: The Royal Thai Navy has ordered the disciplinary detention of a commander for seven days after an investigation found he used excessive force against a naval conscript, in violation of navy regulations and standing orders.

title: Senator to grill health agency over hospital debt
desc: The chair of the Senate health committee plans to scrutinise the finances of the National Health Security Office (NHSO) during debate on the government's policy statement to parliament on April 9-10.

title: Hazardous levels of smog over most of Thailand
desc: Hazardous levels of ultrafine dust shrouded Bangkok and 58 of the 76 provinces late Sunday afternoon with red levels of smog in 10 northern and northeastern provinces, according to the Geo-Informatics and Space Technology Development Agency (Gistda).

title: 中国KOL扎堆打卡是隆！曼谷宋干节今年要这样玩
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，曼谷市挽叻区办事处联合挽叻区发展参与推动委员会，以及多家政府与民间合作单位，共同启动“Songkran Splash Out with Thai Culture 2026”宣传预热活动，提前为今年备受关注的“是隆宋干节”造势升温。

title: Security guard killed in Pattaya crash with Japanese driver
desc: Security guard killed in Pattaya crash with Japanese driver Legacy A security guard died after his motorcycle crashed into an SUV driven by a Japanese restaurant owner who was turning onto a main road in Pattaya early today, April 5.

title: Vendor splashed with hot oil by another worker in Krabi mall
desc: Vendor splashed with hot oil by another worker in Krabi mall Legacy A drink vendor was injured yesterday, April 4, after another worker threw hot cooking oil at her in a shopping mall in Mueang district, Krabi.

title: 泰国暂停原棕榈油出口一年
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，泰国政府通过政宪公报网站发布公告，宣布自2026年4月7日起，禁止向境外出口原棕榈油，为期一年，以稳定国内市场供应与价格。

title: Cambodian worker dies fixing roof at Sri Racha factory
desc: Cambodian worker dies fixing roof at Sri Racha factory Legacy A 38 year old Cambodian man died after falling from a roof he was repairing at a wooden furniture factory in Sri Racha district, Chon Buri.

title: Trailer truck ploughs into traffic, injures 16 in Chachoengsao
desc: Trailer truck ploughs into traffic, injures 16 in Chachoengsao Legacy A 22-wheel trailer truck crashed into several vehicles on the Burapha Withi expressway in Bang Samak subdistrict, Bang Pakong district, Chachoengsao.

title: Australian climbs Pattaya balcony after dispute with Thai partner
desc: Australian climbs Pattaya balcony after dispute with Thai partner Legacy An Australian man was rescued after hanging from a third-floor hotel balcony in Pattaya , yesterday, April 4, after a domestic dispute led to what police described as another apparent bid for attention . Police and tourist officers responded to reports that a foreign man was threatening to jump from a hotel in Soi Buakhao. They found the 59 year old Australian hanging outside a third-floor balcony of the four-storey building. Hotel staff and police attempted to persuade him to return inside. He initially appeared to coope

title: Gang poses as officers to steal motorcycle in Pattaya raid
desc: Gang poses as officers to steal motorcycle in Pattaya raid Legacy Seven individuals allegedly impersonating narcotics officers stormed a home in Pattaya late on April 4, threatening a couple at gunpoint and knife-point before making off with a motorcycle. The incident took place at 10.46pm in the Nong Prue subdistrict of Pattaya . The group of seven claimed to be officers from the Office of the Narcotics Control Board (ONCB) as they raided the property. They left with a green Yamaha Forza motorcycle bearing a Chon Buri registration plate numbered 5 กค 4177. The victims, 30 year old tattoo arti

title: Trat farm worker killed by wild elephant in musth
desc: Trat farm worker killed by wild elephant in musth Legacy A 63 year old Mon farm worker was trampled by a wild elephant in Trat today, April 5, after trying to chase it away . Officials said a strong musth scent was detected at the scene. The incident happened in Bo Phloi subdistrict, Bo Rai district. Rescue workers and local officials went to the scene, where the body was found in a forested area inside Khlong Kaeo Waterfall National Park. Officials quickly removed the body from the forest. The body was later taken to Bo Rai Hospital for relatives to collect for religious rites. The victim’s s

title: 曼谷高温预警：体感温度已达危险级！
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，曼谷环境部门发布预警称，今日曼谷城市热指数已升至“危险”级别，提醒公众注意防暑降温，减少高温时段外出活动。 官方建议，公众需密切关注自身健康状况，如出现异常及时就医，尽量避免在11:00-15:00高温时段外出活动，以降低中暑风险。 对于高风险人群，包括儿童、65岁以上老年人、孕妇、慢性病患者、长期户外工作者、户外运动人群及游客，建议进一步限制户外活动时间，随时观察身体反应，如有不适应立即就医。 根据官方说明，当热指数处于42.0°C至51.9°C之间时，属于“危险”等级；若超过52°C，则进入“极度危险”等级，将对人体健康造成严重威胁。 相关部门呼吁公众合理安排出行时间，避免长时间暴露在高温环境中，并注意补充水分，以应对持续高温天气。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：pptvhd36） The post 曼谷高温预警：体感温度已达危险级！ appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 阿努廷“专业内阁”遭质疑，民众信任度低迷
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月5日，泰国国家发展管理学院民调中心NIDA Poll发布主题为“专业部长能否掌控局势？”的最新民调结果。调查结果显示，在政府引入外部专业人士担任内阁部长应对经济与能源危机的背景下，多数民众对其能力缺乏信心。 调查显示，针对内阁三名副总理兼部长（外部专业人士），受访者整体呈现不信任倾向： 副总理兼外交部长西哈萨 · 不信任：30.23% · 不太信任：29.54% · 较为信任：22.82% · 非常信任：16.03% · 不回答/不知道：1.38% 副总理兼商业部长素帕琪 · 不信任：33.89% · 不太信任：28.70% · 较为信任：21.91% · 非常信任：14.35% · 不回答/不知道：1.15% 副总理兼财政部长艾尼迪 · 不信任：40.38% · 不太信任：37.10% · 较为信任：14.12% · 非常信任：7.10% · 不回答/不知道：1.30% 其中，副总理兼财政部长的不信任比例最高，显示民众对其应对经济问题的信心相对较弱。 针对总理兼内政部长阿努廷在应对经济与能源危机方面所作出的努力，超半数受访者表示“不同情”“不太同情”，整体负面评价高于正面。 分析指出，尽管政府尝试引入具备专业背景的官员以增强治理能力，但在当前经济与能源压力持续的情况下，公众信任仍未明显提升，政策成效与社会沟通仍面临考验。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来

title: Songkhla orders probe after 100,000 litres of diesel found
desc: Songkhla orders probe after 100,000 litres of diesel found Legacy Officials in Songkhla found 100,000 litres of diesel stored at a company’s international truck yard in Sadao district yesterday, April 4, after no documents were produced to back earlier claims that the site had a valid fuel storage permit . The case emerged after social media posts alleged that a Malaysian transport operator with a trailer yard for cross-border runs between Thailand and Malaysia had stockpiled a large amount of fuel. Officials in the area inspected the site and alerted administrative officials and police, whose

title: 以油换油！泰国Bangchak推出“厨房废油换汽车燃油”计划
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月4日，泰国Bangchak集团宣布启动试点项目“Fry to Fly - 2升换1升”，鼓励民众将家庭使用过的废弃食用油用于兑换汽车燃油。根据规则，民众携带2升废弃食用油，即可兑换1升柴油或普通等级汽油，可在参与活动的加油站现场直接加注。 据悉，该项目将于2026年4月6日至30日在曼谷及周边地区的15家Bangchak加油站试行。每人兑换上限为20升废弃食用油（可换约10升汽车燃油）。 据介绍，此举是在原有“Fry to Fly”项目基础上的延伸。此前，该项目主要将回收的废弃食用油用于生产可持续航空燃料。而本次试点则进一步贴近民生，让家庭废油可直接转化为日常出行所需能源。 Bangchak方面表示，该措施亦是企业在当前中东局势紧张、能源价格波动背景下，帮助消费者缓解燃油成本压力的一项具体行动，同时推动资源循环利用 with 绿色能源发展。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：thairath） The post 以油换油！泰国Bangchak推出“厨房废油换汽车燃油”计划 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .
